summary: Removed a committed build-output log file, `errors.log`, and added a broad `*.log` ignore rule to `.gitignore` so future local build logs are not tracked. This was repository hygiene rather than decompilation logic work: it reduced noise from generated/local artifacts and prevented accidental re-commits of machine-specific build output.
what_changed: Deleted the tracked root-level `errors.log` file, removing 309 lines of captured local build output.
Added `*.log` to `.gitignore` near other generated/context/build artifacts such as `ctx.c`, `ctx_includes.h`, and `expected/`.

The removed log contained local path information and transient build progress output, including `NON_MATCHING=0`, `GENERATE_MAP=1`, `EPILOGUE_PROCESS=1`, compile/assemble steps, and final progress text.
smart_moves: Fixed the immediate tracked artifact and the recurrence path in the same PR: deleting `errors.log` alone would allow future logs to be accidentally committed again.
Used a general `*.log` ignore pattern instead of only `errors.log`, covering similar future tool or build logs.
Kept the change minimal and isolated to repository hygiene with no source or build-system behavior changes.
decomp_lessons: Do not commit local build logs or progress captures; they are noisy, machine-specific, and can contain local filesystem paths.
When removing an accidentally tracked generated file, add an ignore rule at the same time so the cleanup persists.
Decomp repositories generate many transient artifacts during matching and verification; `.gitignore` should actively protect the source tree from build/run output churn.
naming_conventions: The PR treats `.log` files as generated/local artifacts rather than source assets.
No symbol, function, struct, or header naming convention was introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactic changed in this PR.
The deleted log shows the existing workflow emitted compile, assemble, link, `elf2dol`, progress, and expected-sync output, but the PR only removed that captured output from version control.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that remove generated files and add corresponding `.gitignore` rules.
Search for remaining tracked `.log` files or other local build artifacts in the repository history.
Look for later `.gitignore` changes around build outputs, `expected/`, context files, or decomp tooling artifacts.
